questions.question: Q1. What environmental factor determines the raw energy available for conversion by the solar photovoltaic fields?

explanations.a: Correct. Solar irradiance represents the power per unit area received from the sun, directly determining solar panel generation.
explanations.b: Ambient temperature affects efficiency, but is not the raw input energy source.
explanations.c: Blade pitch angle is a wind turbine control parameter.
explanations.d: Wind speed determines wind turbine output, not solar.

questions.question: Q2. Below what wind speed threshold (cut-in speed) does the wind turbine generation drop to 0 kW?

explanations.a: Turbines require a minimum wind threshold to overcome friction and spin the rotor.
explanations.b: Correct. Below the cut-in wind speed of 3.0 m/s, the wind turbine does not generate any power.
explanations.c: 12.0 m/s is the rated wind speed where the turbine achieves maximum output.
explanations.d: 20.0 m/s is the cut-out wind speed where the turbine shuts down for safety.

questions.question: Q3. What happens to wind turbine operation when wind speed exceeds the cut-out limit of 20 m/s?

answers.a: Output is capped at its rated limit of 600 kW
answers.b: The rotor enters overspeed mode, increasing generation
answers.c: Mechanical brakes engage (output falls to 0 kW) to prevent structural damage
answers.d: The system automatically switches the biomass plant off

explanations.a: Output does not remain at rated; it drops to 0 kW for safety.
explanations.b: Overspeeding is dangerous and is actively prevented.
explanations.c: Correct. At 20 m/s and above, the turbine is braked to protect it from extreme wind forces.
explanations.d: Biomass operation is independent of wind turbine safety braking.

questions.question: Q4. How does adjusting the blade pitch angle affect the wind turbine's power generation?

answers.b: It reduces turbine output by turning the blades out of the wind
answers.c: It increases the wind speed cut-out limit
answers.d: It charges the backup utility grid directly

explanations.a: Increasing pitch angle reduces aerodynamic lift and efficiency.
explanations.b: Correct. Adjusting/increasing the blade pitch angle reduces aerodynamic power extraction to regulate or reduce output.
explanations.c: The cut-out wind speed threshold is constant at 20 m/s.
explanations.d: Blade pitch is a mechanical control and does not charge the grid directly.

questions.question: Q5. What is the nominal capacity rating of the Biomass Plant when it is online at 100% fuel rate?

explanations.a: 200 kW is the output at a 50% fuel rate.
explanations.b: Correct. The Biomass Plant has a maximum rating of 400 kW at a 100% fuel rate.
explanations.d: The biomass system capacity does not exceed 400 kW.

questions.question: Q6. In an Islanded (off-grid) microgrid connection mode, what happens to surplus generated renewable energy that cannot be absorbed by loads or storage?

answers.a: It is automatically exported to the utility grid
answers.b: It is stored in the hydrogen tank indefinitely, bypassing limits
answers.c: It is curtailed (wasted) because there is no grid connection to absorb it

explanations.a: In Islanded mode, the grid is disconnected, so export is impossible.
explanations.b: Hydrogen storage cannot exceed its physical tank capacity (20 kg).
explanations.c: Correct. In Islanded mode, excess power that cannot be stored must be curtailed.
explanations.d: Surplus power charges the battery, not discharges it.

questions.question: Q7. What is the minimum state of charge (SoC) limit for the Lithium Battery below which discharge is blocked to protect the battery cell?

explanations.a: Discharging to 0% causes permanent degradation to lithium chemistry.
explanations.b: Correct. The battery discharge is capped at a minimum 10.0% SoC limit.
explanations.c: The minimum SoC cut-off is 10.0%, not 20.0%.
explanations.d: Batteries can discharge way below 50.0% SoC during deficits.

questions.question: Q8. What is the primary function of the hydrogen electrolyzer in this experiment?

answers.a: To burn hydrogen gas to generate steam power
answers.b: To convert excess electrical energy into stored hydrogen gas
answers.c: To supply power when demand exceeds generation

explanations.a: The electrolyzer uses electricity to split water; it does not burn hydrogen.
explanations.b: Correct. The electrolyzer uses surplus power to generate and store hydrogen.
explanations.c: The fuel cell performs energy discharge, not the electrolyzer.
explanations.d: The electrolyzer acts purely as a deferrable energy storage load.

questions.question: Q9. What is the maximum input power capacity of the hydrogen electrolyzer in this EMS system?

explanations.a: The electrolyzer input limit is larger than 50 kW.
explanations.b: The limit is 200 kW, not 100 kW.
explanations.c: Correct. The electrolyzer is capped at a maximum charging rate of 200 kW.
explanations.d: The input charging rate is restricted to a 200 kW limit.

questions.question: Q10. Which dispatch strategy focuses on keeping the battery SoC at 100% at all times to prepare for grid outages?

explanations.a: Max Self-Consumption prioritizes using battery power to minimize export/import.
explanations.b: Peak Grid Shaving holds battery reserve unless grid import limits are breached.
explanations.c: Correct. Battery Backup Support prioritizes keeping the battery fully charged (100% SoC) in case of grid failure.
explanations.d: Demand Response sheds load, rather than managing battery backup thresholds.
